confidence_rationale: Video is classified as instructional/marketing content, not an actual FLS task performance. No scoring is appropriate. Content shows multiple task types in brief demonstrations (grasping, suturing) but does not constitute a complete, scorable performance of any single FLS task. Video is primarily educational narration with overlaid text, equipment demonstration, and research validation material.
cannot_determine: Task ID — multiple FLS tasks are referenced but not individually performed
Completion time — no single task is fully executed
Phase timings — content is segmented by instructional topic, not task phases
Penalties — no trainee performance to evaluate
technique_summary: This is an instructional and marketing video for the Surgical Science LapSim Certification Program. It presents program structure, research validation, and brief skill demonstrations, but does not contain a scorable individual FLS task performance.

demonstrated_techniques: Laparoscopic grasping technique with needle driver and grasper
verbal_cues: Program emphasizes skill-based learning rather than time/volume-based training
Training progresses from 40 expert training tasks to Ahlberg EXAM certification (12 tasks, proficiency required 2× per task)
Research shows residents trained on LapSim achieved 60% reduction in surgical errors and 40% reduction in operating time on first 10 cholecystectomies
Benchmark against surgeons with 300+ cholecystectomy experience
common_errors_addressed: The program avoids time-based progression; instead emphasizes demonstrated competency
Clinical validation ensures training transfers to operating room performance

note: No FLS score can be assigned to this video. It is promotional and educational material for the LapSim program, not a performance evaluation.
